channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korea’s ruling party chief shows support for suspending presidential powers of Yoon
item.description: South Korea’s ruling party chief says it’s necessary to suspend the constitutional powers of President Yoon Suk Yeol.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why the rebel capture of Syria's Hama, a city with a dark history, matters
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — One of the darkest moments in the modern history of the Arab world happened more than four decades ago, when then-Syrian President Hafez Assad launched what came to be known as the Hama Massacre.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bangladesh court bans publication of speeches by ousted Prime Minister Hasina
item.description: A special tribunal in Bangladesh has banned the publication of any speeches by former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina, who is in exile in India after being ousted in August after mass protests.
item.dc:creator: Julhas Alam, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hamas official says Gaza ceasefire talks have resumed after weekslong hiatus
item.description: A Hamas official says that after a weekslong hiatus international mediators have resumed negotiating with the militant group and Israel over a ceasefire in Gaza, and that he was hopeful a deal to end the 14-month war was within reach.
item.dc:creator: Abby Sewell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cuba restores power grid and resumes planned blackouts of about 5 hours a day
item.description: After a nationwide blackout left millions without electricity for several hours in Cuba, the power grid has been restored, authorities said, adding that scheduled power outages will now resume.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Rodríguez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Syrian insurgents capture central city of Hama in severe setback to the Syrian president
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Syrian insurgents swept into the central city of Hama on Thursday and government forces withdrew, dealing another major blow to Syrian President Bashar Assad days after insurgents captured much of Aleppo, the country’s largest city.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Germany hands over Australian ancestral remains held by museums for over 100 years
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Five sets of ancestral remains from Australia that had been in German museum collections since the 19th century were handed back at a ceremony Thursday that a community representative described as a sad but “very joyful” moment.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Qatar returns to Hamas-Israel negotiations as Trump envoy looks to make inroads
item.description: Qatar has once again resumed its top role in helping negotiate a cease-fire between Israel and Hamas to return hostages in Gaza, as President-elect Donald Trump's Middle East envoy begins meeting with top leaders amid crisis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 12-year-old girl finds ancient Egyptian amulet in Israel
item.description: An ordinary family trip turned extraordinary when a 12-year-old girl found what turned out to be an ancient Egyptian amulet dating back 3,500 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kosovo accuses Serbia of 'terrorist attack' resembling Russian actions in Ukraine
item.description: Kosovo accused Serbia of carrying out a terrorist attack on a water pipeline in the Serb majority north of Kosovo. Serbia denies the accusations, but the recent bloodshed highlights that peace in the Balkans is far from certain.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yoon replaces the defense minister as South Korea's parliament moves to vote on their impeachments
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s president replaced his defense minister Thursday as opposition parties moved to impeach both men over the stunning-but-brief imposition of martial law that brought armed troops into Seoul streets.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: International students confused, anxious about their futures under second Trump term
item.description: International students are holding their breath as President-elect Trump gears up to return to the White House. Multiple schools have put out guidance on when foreign-born students should and shouldn't go abroad to ensure they are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Alleged Chinese cyberattack: FBI urges encrypted apps
item.description: Salt Typhoon, a Chinese government-linked hacking group, is one of the largest intelligence breaches in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: What's next: South Korean leader's martial law declaration threatens his political career
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — President Yoon Suk Yeol’s stunning martial law declaration lasted just hours, but experts say it raised serious questions about his ability to govern for the remaining 2 1/2 years of his term and whether he will abide by democratic principles.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president's martial law declaration a 'blunder,' could embolden North Korea, expert says
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law and now faces impeachment over the surprising move from opposition lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 hours of anger, bravery and defiance during martial law in South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol shocked the nation when he declared martial law and called in troops to secure the National Assembly building where parliament sits.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amnesty International says Israel is committing genocide in Gaza. Israel rejects the allegations
item.description: Amnesty International accuses Israel of committing genocide in the Gaza Strip during its war with Hamas, saying it has sought to deliberately destroy Palestinians by mounting deadly attacks, demolishing vital infrastructure and preventing the delivery of humanitarian aid.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Takeaways from Biden's Africa trip: Pardon of son Hunter overshadows official business
item.description: LUANDA, Angola (AP) — President Joe Biden's long-delayed trip to Africa had many of the hallmarks of a traditional state visit: There was a 12-shot cannon salute. A series of warm handshakes with Angolan President João Lourenço. Celebratory music. Photo opportunities.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico seizes record amount of fentanyl days after Trump issued tariff threats
item.description: Mexican troops seized a record amount of fentanyl pills in the state of Sinaloa on Tuesday, which broke a record, authorities said this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French government collapses after no-confidence vote passes against Prime Minister Michel Barnier
item.description: Prime Minister Michel Barnier is expected to resign after the National Assembly passed a vote of no-confidence on Wednesday, effectively collapsing the current government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A major power plant fails in Cuba, plunging the island into darkness — again
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — A new, widespread power outage plunged Cuba into darkness on Wednesday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, leaving millions without electricity and forcing authorities to suspend classes and work activities indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Monica Crowley for senior State Department post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday nominated Monica Crowley, a former Fox News contributor who faced public scrutiny during Trump’s first term over allegations of plagiarism, for a top public relations post in the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump's FBI pick Kash Patel targeted in Iranian cyberattack
item.description: Kash Patel, President-elect Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, was among those targeted by an Iranian cyber attack. It’s unclear whether any information was gained through the attack, but it makes Patel the latest figure in the Trump campaign to be pursued by the nation.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Hamas issues instructions to 'neutralize' hostages if Israel attempts rescue operation
item.description: Hamas threatened to "neutralize" its hostages if Israel attempted another rescue operation, according to an internal statement seen by Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iranian activist, Nobel prize winner Mohammadi released from jail for medical treatment
item.description: Human rights activist Narges Mohammadi, who is imprisoned in Iran on charges including spreading propaganda, had her jail term suspended to undergo medical treatment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hispanic senators press Biden to protect immigrants before leaving White House
item.description: The Senate’s three current Hispanic Democrats are calling on President Biden to extend and strengthen humanitarian protections for certain groups of immigrants whose legal status could be stripped by the incoming Trump administration. In a letter on Wednesday, Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto (Nev.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Archaeologists in Denmark discover over 100 weapons from the Iron Age during excavations
item.description: Archaeologists in Denmark unearthed what they've suggested is a sacrifice of weapons. The weapons likely belonged to a powerful chieftain thousands of years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with Hungary’s effort to end Holocaust survivor lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday probed the international consequences of allowing a group of Holocaust survivors and their heirs to leverage U.S. courts to seek compensation from Hungary for confiscating their property.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu reacts after Trump warns of 'hell to pay' if Hamas doesn't free hostages
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says President-elect Trump has the emphasis in "the right place" with his latest demand for Hamas to release hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chicago welcomes eight children from Gaza for medical treatment
item.description: Over the next few months, the children will receive medical treatment at U.S. hospitals, free of cost.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: Israel warns it will go after Lebanon directly if cease-fire with Hezbollah collapses
item.description: Israel issued a warning at Lebanon that it will not long distinguish between Beirut and Hezbollah should the cease-fire between Jerusalem and the terrorist group collapse following an exchange of strikes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean parliament votes to defy president by lifting his declaration of martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law late Tuesday, vowing to eliminate “anti-state” forces as he struggles against an opposition that controls the country’s parliament and that he accuses of sympathizing with communist North Korea.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Palestinians say Hamas, Fatah close to agreement on committee for postwar Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Palestinian officials say Fatah and Hamas are closing in on an agreement to appoint a committee of politically independent technocrats to administer the Gaza Strip after the war. It would effectively end Hamas' rule and could help advance ceasefire talks with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 11 in Lebanon after fire exchanges with Hezbollah that test ceasefire’s limits
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel unleashed its largest wave of airstrikes across Lebanon since agreeing to a ceasefire with Hezbollah last week, killing at least 11 people on Monday after the Lebanese militant group fired a volley of projectiles as a warning over what it said were Israeli truce violations.
item.dc:creator: NATALIE MELTZER and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elton John says he has lost his eyesight, struggles to see new musical
item.description: Elton John says he struggled to watch his new musical because he has lost his eyesight after contracting an infection.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas releases new hostage video of American-Israeli Edan Alexander
item.description: Hamas released a propaganda video Saturday showing the 20-year-old Alexander alive, marking his first proof of life since being kidnapped.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation
